author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
hari he!
tomāra je śuddhabhakta, tomāte se anurakta,
bhukti⋅mukti tuccha kari’ jāne
bāreka dekhite tava, cidākāra⋅śrī⋅vaibhava,
tṛṇa bali’ anya sukha māne

se saba bhaktera saṅge, līlā kara nānāraṅge,
viraha sahite nāhi pāra
kṛpā kari’ akiñcane, dekhāo mahātmagaṇe,
sādhu vinā gati nāhi āra

se⋅bhaktacaraṇa⋅dhana, kabe pā’ba daraśana,
śodhiba āmāra duṣṭa mana
e bhaktivinoda bhaṇe, kṛpā ha’be jatakṣaṇe,
mahātmāra ha’be daraśana
en_translation:
O Hari! Your pure devotee, being deeply attached to You, considers material enjoyment and liberation to be insignificant. Having seen Your spiritual opulence even once, they consider all other pleasures as mere grass.

You perform various pastimes with such devotees and cannot tolerate separation from them. Out of mercy, please show such great souls to this destitute person; without the saintly, there is no other path.

When will I behold the wealth of the lotus feet of those devotees and thus purify my wicked mind? Bhaktivinoda says: only when mercy descends will I have the vision of those great souls.
